content.full_text:
THE LAW
OFFICES OF
ROBERT
J LOGAN

152 NORTH THIRD STREET
SUITE 201 • SAN JOSE, CA 95112
TELEPHONE • 408.287.2156
FACSIMILE • 408.280.1749

Robert J. Logan
Cecilia M. Quick
Dick A. Schouten

Ms. Susan Bobrow
Legal Division
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95814

March 15, 1991

Re: Request for Opinion/Brisbane Acres

Dear Ms. Bobrow:

You have requested further details regarding the specific
property interests of the various public officials that own
property within 300 yards of the Brisbane Acres subdivision.
This information will supplement the map we provided you
earlier showing the lots and listing the owners of those
lots.

Several lots which exist were created in the late 1930's
and are presently undersized and not legally buildable lots.
The minimum required lot size in Brisbane is 5,000 square
feet. Some of the lots you see on the map are only 2,500
square feet. Consequently, property owners often merge the
smaller lots thereby creating a single lot of adequate size.

Below is a recap of the various lots owned by public
officials in the Brisbane Acres.

OFFICIAL                PROPERTY               INTEREST

MAYOR WALDO            BLOCK 56; LOT           RESIDENCE
                       27,28,29

MAYOR WALDO            BLOCK 56; LOT           INVESTMENT
                       24,25,26

COUNCILMEMBER
JOHNSON                BLOCK 47: LOT 18        RESIDENCE

PLANNING COMM.         JOY AVENUE LOT          INVESTMENT
QUILTER                11

                                    1

PLANNING COMM.          BLOCK  47:  LOT      RESIDENCE
PETERSON                16, 17

PARKS COMM.            BLOCK 45: LOT 3         RESIDENCE
APOTHEKER

PARKS COMM.            BLOCK   50;  LOT        RESIDENCE
DAVIS                  5,6

PARKS COMM.            BLOCK   44;  LOT        RESIDENCE
GROUNDWATER            9,10

CITY    MANAGER        BLOCK   52;  LOT        RESIDENCE
LEITER                 7,8

CITY    MANAGER        BLOCK  56:  LOTS        INVESTMENT
LEITER                 21,22,23


          If you have any further questions, please let me know.

                                                Very truly yours,

                                                [signature]
                                                Robert J. Logan


RJL:jjb
cc:Mayor Waldo
   Councilmember Johnson
   City Manager Leiter
   Quilter
   Peterson
   Apotheker
   Davis
   Groundwater




                                    2

THE LAW
OFFICES OF

ROBERT
J LOGAN

152 NORTH THIRD STREET
SUITE 201 • SAN JOSE, CA 95112
TELEPHONE • 408.287.2156
FACSIMILE • 408.280.1749

Robert J. Logan
Cecilia M. Quick
Dick A. Schouten

April 16, 1991

Susan Bobrow, Esq.
Legal Division
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street
Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re: Request for Extension of Time Rendering Formal Written Opinion

Dear Ms. Bobrow:

This letter confirms your request and our agreement to extend the time for rendering an
opinion on Brisbane Acres for an additional two weeks. We will anticipate receiving your opinion
on April 30, 1991.

If you need more information, do not hesitate to call.

Sincerely yours,

Robert J. Logan

RJL/M
enclosure
cc: Mayor Waldo
Councilmember Johnson
City Manager

THE LAW
OFFICES OF

ROBERT
J LOGAN

152 NORTH THIRD STREET
SUITE 201 • SAN JOSE, CA 95112
TELEPHONE • 408.287.2156
FACSIMILE • 408.280.1749

March 1, 1991

Susan Bobrow, Esq.
Legal Division
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street
Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re: Request for Formal Written Advice Dated February 12, 1991/Supplemental Information

Dear Ms. Bobrow:

On February 12, 1991, I made a formal request for a written opinion from the Fair Political Practices Commission. You have subsequently requested more specific information regarding the names and official capacities of those individuals seeking the advice. You have also requested information regarding the location of their property in relation to the Acres.

I have enclosed a map which shows the names, official status and location of the various individuals that might be affected. The orange line represents the borders of the Acres. The arrows point to the specific property interests of the individuals requesting the opinion.

The request for opinion precedes any specific decisions made with reference to the Acres. The decisions that will be processed by the Council, Boards and Commission of which the specific individuals are members relate to land use considerations, which include changes to the general plan, possible rezonings, and determinations regarding requirements for infrastructure such as roads, water, sewer, fire and police protection.

I hope this additional information completes your file for purposes of responding to the request. If you need more information, do not hesitate to call.

Sincerely yours,

Robert J. Logan

RJL/kl
enclosure
cc: Mayor Waldo
Councilmember Johnson
City Manager
